slide_002.appears_in: Introduction to Organizational Behaviour MN 4063: Organizational Behaviour & Management

slide_007.appears_in: Work provides a source of social status in the community.
Work can be an important source of identity, self-esteem, and, for some, a means for self-actualisation.
Work can be an important source of identity, self-esteem, and, for some, a means for self-actualisation.

slide_008.appears_in: Work and Self- Esteem Work contributes to self-esteem in at least two ways.
1. It provides individuals with an opportunity to demonstrate competence or mastery over themselves and their environment.
1. It provides individuals with an opportunity to demonstrate competence or mastery over themselves and their environment.

slide_009.appears_in: Alienation If your work doesn't provide you with any of these functions, you will often experience sensations of powerlessness, meaninglessness, and normlessness—a condition called alienation.
Alienation If your work doesn't provide you with any of these functions, you will often experience sensations of powerlessness, meaninglessness, and normlessness—a condition called alienation.
Alienation If your work doesn't provide you with any of these functions, you will often experience sensations of powerlessness, meaninglessness, and normlessness—a condition called alienation.
Alienation If your work doesn't provide you with any of these functions, you will often experience sensations of powerlessness, meaninglessness, and normlessness—a condition called alienation.

slide_010.appears_in: What is OB? Organizational behaviour (OB) is a field of study that investigates the impact individuals, groups, and structure have on behaviour within organizations, to apply such knowledge toward improving an organization's effectiveness.

slide_011.appears_in: OB in Simple Terms Simply, OB is the study of what people do in an organization and how their behaviour affects the organization's performance. OB's goal is to understand and predict human behaviour i

slide_012.appears_in: OB in Simple Terms The complexities of human behaviour are not easy to predict, but neither are they random—certain fundamental consistencies underlie the behaviour of all individuals.
OB studies three determinants of behaviour in organizations: individuals, groups, and structure.
OB in Simple Terms The complexities of human behaviour are not easy to predict, but neither are they random—certain fundamental consistencies underlie the behaviour of all individuals.
OB studies three determinants of behaviour in organizations: individuals, groups, and structure.

slide_014.appears_in: Multidisciplinary Nature of OB OB is an <u>applied behavioural science</u> built on contributions from several behavioural disciplines, mainly psychology and social psychology, sociology, anthropology, and, increasingly, political science and neuroscience.

Multidisciplinary Nature of OB OB is an <u>applied behavioural science</u> built on contributions from several behavioural disciplines, mainly psychology and social psychology, sociology, anthropology, and, increasingly, political science and neuroscience.

slide_015.appears_in: lan af ¢ Sociology explores how society shapes people.

slide_017.appears_in: Anthropology examines rites and rituals that shape how groups and cultures work.
Political science examines the role of power within organizations.
Multidisciplinary Nature of OB Social psychology bridges sociology and psychology, and tries to understand the impact the group has on the outlook of the individual.

slide_018.appears_in: Beginnings of OB Organizational behaviour as a distinct discipline started when scientific approaches to management began to indicate that behavioural considerations were important predictors of productivity.

Beginnings of OB Organizational behaviour as a distinct discipline started when scientific approaches to management began to indicate that behavioural considerations were important predictors of productivity.

slide_022.appears_in: Employees performed better when i managers treated them in a_ positive eS manner ( Human relations).

slide_025.appears_in: Hawthorne effect: Subjects behaved differently because of the active participation of the researchers in the Hawthorne experiments.

slide_026.appears_in: Absolutes in OB Laws in the physical sciences—chemistry, astronomy, physics—are consistent and apply in various situations.

slide_033.appears_in: Management as an Integrating Activity Whatever the individual's orientation to work, the nature of the work organisation or cultural influences, it is through the process of management that the efforts of members of the organisation are coordinated, directed and guided towards the achievement of its goals.

Management as an Integrating Activity Whatever the individual's orientation to work, the nature of the work organisation or cultural influences, it is through the process of management that the efforts of members of the organisation are coordinated, directed and guided towards the achievement of its goals.

slide_035.appears_in: Management as an Integrating Activity At the heart of successful management is the problem of integrating the individual and the organisation, and this requires an understanding of both human personality and work organisations.

Management as an Integrating Activity At the heart of successful management is the problem of integrating the individual and the organisation, and this requires an understanding of both human personality and work organisations.

slide_038.appears_in: Implications for Managers OB can improve your employees' work quality and productivity by showing you how to empower your employees, design and implement change programs, improve customer service, and

Implications for Managers OB can improve your employees' work quality and productivity by showing you how to empower your employees, design and implement change programs, improve customer service, and

Implications for Managers OB can improve your employees' work quality and productivity by showing you how to empower your employees, design and implement change programs, improve customer service, and

Implications for Managers OB can improve your employees' work quality and productivity by showing you how to empower your employees, design and implement change programs, improve customer service, and help your employees balance work-life conflicts.
Use caution when applying OB research in international contexts; some of the underlying cultural norms and expectations may vary, altering results.

slide_039.appears_in: Implications for Managers Recognize that human behaviour in the workplace is influenced by factors at three different levels: individual, group, and organizational.

Implications for Managers Recognize that human behaviour in the workplace is influenced by factors at three different levels: individual, group, and organizational. Paying attention to relevant factor
